source: Flocabulary G5 + Wordly Wise Book 5 + State Standards

words.definition: to study something carefully by looking at its parts
words.example: Analyze the graph to find which month had the most rain.

words.definition: to judge how good, important, or useful something is
words.example: The judges will evaluate each project at the science fair.

words.definition: to explain what something means in your own words
words.example: How do you interpret the ending of the story?

words.definition: to notice the difference between two or more things
words.example: Can you distinguish between a frog and a toad?

words.definition: important enough to be noticed or to have an effect
words.example: The discovery of penicillin was a significant moment in medicine.

words.definition: closely connected to the topic or situation
words.example: Only include relevant details in your report.

words.definition: a particular way of looking at or thinking about something
words.example: Try to see the problem from your friend's perspective.

words.definition: the standards used to judge or decide something
words.example: The criteria for the award include grades and community service.

words.definition: something you believe to be true without checking
words.example: Don't make assumptions — ask questions first.

words.definition: based on good reasons; logical and fair
words.example: She made a valid point during the debate.

words.definition: a system of government where people choose their leaders by voting
words.example: In a democracy, every adult citizen can vote.

words.definition: the basic set of laws that governs a country
words.example: The U.S. Constitution protects freedom of speech.

words.definition: the power or right to give orders and make decisions
words.example: The principal has the authority to change the school rules.

words.definition: the freedom to live, think, and act as you choose
words.example: The Statue of Liberty is a symbol of freedom.

words.definition: to officially control and manage a country or area
words.example: Elected officials govern the state.

words.definition: the system of making, selling, and buying things in a country
words.example: A strong economy means more people have jobs.

words.definition: an official rule or plan made by a government or group
words.example: The school's new policy requires uniforms.

words.definition: the fair treatment of all people
words.example: The lawyer fought for justice for the wrongly accused man.

words.definition: a person who comes to live in a new country
words.example: My grandparents were immigrants from Korea.

words.definition: a change made to improve something
words.example: The education reform helped more students succeed.

words.definition: the layer of gases that surrounds the Earth
words.example: The atmosphere protects us from the sun's harmful rays.

words.definition: the force that pulls things toward the center of the Earth
words.example: Gravity is the reason a ball falls back down when you throw it up.

words.definition: to travel in a curved path around a larger object in space
words.example: The Earth orbits the sun once every year.

words.definition: the spinning of an object around its own center
words.example: The Earth's rotation causes day and night.

words.definition: the slow wearing away of rock or soil by water or wind
words.example: Erosion from the river carved out the canyon over millions of years.

words.definition: a natural solid substance found in the ground
words.example: Gold and silver are valuable minerals.

words.definition: all the living and nonliving things in an area that work together
words.example: A pond ecosystem includes fish, plants, water, and sunlight.

words.definition: a group of living things that are very similar and can have babies together
words.example: The bald eagle is a protected species in America.

words.definition: a large region with a specific climate and living things
words.example: The tropical rainforest is the most diverse biome on Earth.

words.definition: able to be replaced naturally and used again
words.example: Solar power is a renewable source of energy.

words.definition: the result of an action, especially a bad one
words.example: Forgetting your homework has consequences.

words.definition: the result or effect of an action or event
words.example: The outcome of the experiment surprised everyone.

words.definition: one of the things that causes or influences a result
words.example: Hard work is an important factor in success.

words.definition: the final result of an action or event
words.example: The outcome of the game was a surprise to everyone.

words.definition: to produce a change in something
words.example: A lack of sleep can affect your ability to concentrate.

words.definition: to act on each other or communicate with one another
words.example: Animals interact with their environment every day.

words.definition: a good result or advantage from something
words.example: One benefit of reading is a larger vocabulary.

words.definition: to put a limit on something
words.example: The rules restrict the use of phones during class time.

words.definition: to happen or take place
words.example: Earthquakes can occur without any warning.

words.definition: something that happens because of something else; a response
words.example: Her reaction to the surprise party was pure joy.

words.definition: the way something is built, organized, or put together
words.example: The structure of the essay includes an introduction, body, and conclusion.

words.definition: to arrange things into groups based on shared features
words.example: Scientists classify animals into groups like mammals and reptiles.

words.definition: a group of things that share something in common
words.example: Sort the animals into categories: mammals, birds, and reptiles.

words.definition: the order in which things happen or are arranged
words.example: Put the events of the story in the correct sequence.

words.definition: to put things into groups based on what they have in common
words.example: The librarian categorized the new books by subject.

words.definition: an important part or quality of something
words.example: The best feature of the new phone is its camera.

words.definition: the purpose or job of something
words.example: The function of the heart is to pump blood through the body.

words.definition: made up of many connected parts; not simple
words.example: The human brain is the most complex organ in the body.

words.definition: a simple drawing that shows how something works or is organized
words.example: Use the diagram to understand how blood flows through the heart.

words.definition: one part of something larger; a basic substance in chemistry
words.example: Surprise is an important element of a good mystery story.

words.definition: a statement that something is true, which may need proof
words.example: The advertisement's claim that it works instantly seems too good to be true.

words.definition: a formal discussion where people argue different sides
words.example: The class held a debate about whether homework should be banned.

words.definition: to disagree with something or be against it
words.example: Some parents oppose the idea of longer school days.

words.definition: to show or prove that something is right or reasonable
words.example: She justified her answer by showing her work step by step.

words.definition: an unfair preference for or against something
words.example: A good reporter writes without bias.

words.definition: based on clear thinking and good reasoning
words.example: His argument was logical and easy to follow.

words.definition: an argument made against another argument
words.example: A strong essay includes a counterargument to show you considered other views.

words.definition: able to be trusted or believed
words.example: Use credible sources like encyclopedias for your research.

words.definition: a person's public position on an issue
words.example: What is your stance on school uniforms?

words.definition: producing the result that was wanted or intended
words.example: Hand washing is an effective way to prevent illness.

words.definition: a part of the body that has a specific job
words.example: The heart is an organ that pumps blood.

words.definition: the process of eating the right foods to stay healthy
words.example: Good nutrition includes eating fruits and vegetables every day.

words.definition: the smallest living part of a plant, animal, or person
words.example: Every living thing is made up of cells.

words.definition: a sign that shows you might be sick
words.example: A cough and fever are common symptoms of a cold.

words.definition: protected against a disease or illness
words.example: Vaccines help your body become immune to certain diseases.

words.definition: related to the body rather than the mind
words.example: Running and swimming are types of physical exercise.

words.definition: related to the mind and thinking
words.example: Reading puzzles is good for your mental health.

words.definition: a type of matter or material
words.example: Water is a substance that is essential for life.

words.definition: to take in or soak up a liquid or information
words.example: A sponge can absorb a lot of water.

words.definition: to get better after being sick or hurt
words.example: It took two weeks to recover from the flu.

words.definition: the amount of something that is available to use
words.example: The store has a large supply of school notebooks.

words.definition: the desire or need of people to buy or have something
words.example: There is high demand for the new video game.

words.definition: to send goods to another country for sale
words.example: Brazil exports coffee to countries around the world.

words.definition: to bring goods into a country from another country
words.example: The United States imports many products from China.

words.definition: the money earned after all costs are paid
words.example: The lemonade stand made a profit of twenty dollars.

words.definition: to use up or eat something
words.example: Americans consume a lot of energy every year.

words.definition: not enough to meet everyone's needs; hard to find
words.example: Clean water is scarce in some parts of the world.

words.definition: to give out or spread something to several places or people
words.example: Volunteers distribute food to families in need.

words.definition: to make products in large amounts using machines
words.example: This factory manufactures over a million shoes each year.

words.definition: to put money or time into something hoping for a good result
words.example: She decided to invest her savings in education.

words.definition: to represent something else, especially an idea or quality
words.example: A dove symbolizes peace in many cultures.

words.definition: a comparison between two unlike things without using like or as
words.example: Saying 'time is money' is a metaphor.

words.definition: when something happens that is the opposite of what you expect
words.example: It was irony that the fire station burned down.

words.definition: a feeling of stress or worry caused by disagreement
words.example: There was tension between the two teams before the big game.

words.definition: the words spoken between characters in a story or play
words.example: The playwright wrote natural-sounding dialogue for each character.

words.definition: the feeling or mood created by a writer's choice of words
words.example: The tone of the letter was formal and serious.

words.definition: a type or category of literature, music, or art
words.example: Fantasy and mystery are popular book genres.

words.definition: the person or character who tells the story
words.example: The narrator of the story is a ten-year-old girl.

words.definition: a group of lines in a poem, like a paragraph in a story
words.example: The poem has four stanzas, each with five lines.

words.definition: to communicate a message, idea, or feeling
words.example: The artist used bright colors to convey happiness.

words.definition: a new idea, method, or invention that improves something
words.example: The smartphone was one of the greatest innovations of the 21st century.

words.definition: a tool or machine made for a specific purpose
words.example: A tablet is a device used for reading and learning.

words.definition: working well without wasting time or energy
words.example: LED lights are more efficient than regular light bulbs.

words.definition: facts and information collected for study
words.example: Scientists collect data from experiments to test their ideas.

words.definition: the ability to use or get something
words.example: Every student should have access to a computer at school.

words.definition: working by itself without human control
words.example: The automatic doors open when you walk toward them.

words.definition: to make small changes to something to improve it
words.example: The engineer modified the design to make it safer.

words.definition: a group of connected things or people that share information
words.example: The school has a computer network that connects all classrooms.

words.definition: to create a model or imitation of something real
words.example: The flight simulator lets you practice flying a plane safely.

words.definition: to change something into a different form or purpose
words.example: They converted the old barn into a beautiful library.

words.definition: the ability to understand and share another person's feelings
words.example: Showing empathy means you care about how others feel.

words.definition: an agreement where each side gives up something
words.example: They reached a compromise — pizza on Friday, tacos on Saturday.

words.definition: to talk with others to reach an agreement
words.example: The two teams negotiated the rules before the game started.

words.definition: to recognize the value of something or be thankful for it
words.example: I appreciate your help with the heavy boxes.

words.definition: to accept things you may not agree with
words.example: In a diverse classroom, students learn to tolerate different opinions.

words.definition: a serious disagreement or argument
words.example: The dispute between the neighbors was settled by a judge.

words.definition: shared by two or more people equally
words.example: They have a mutual love of soccer.

words.definition: to publicly support or speak in favor of something
words.example: She advocates for recycling programs at every school.

words.definition: including many different types of people or things
words.example: Our school has a diverse group of students from many countries.

words.definition: to organize people or activities to work together smoothly
words.example: The teachers coordinated the field trip schedule carefully.

words.definition: used to compare two different facts or situations
words.example: She enjoys reading, whereas her brother prefers sports.

words.definition: used to add surprising or important information
words.example: I'm not tired. As a matter of fact, I feel great!
collocations: as a matter of fact I
as a matter of fact it

words.definition: representing someone; in the interest of someone
words.example: The captain spoke on behalf of the whole team.

words.definition: to consider something when making a decision
words.example: Take into account the weather before planning an outdoor event.

words.definition: to try hard to do something
words.example: Make an effort to be kind to the new student.

words.definition: to make a final decision after thinking
words.example: After reviewing the evidence, the jury came to a conclusion.

words.definition: to remember something important while doing something
words.example: Keep in mind that the test is next Monday.

words.definition: the good and bad points of something
words.example: List the pros and cons before making your decision.

words.definition: without being affected by something
words.example: Everyone deserves respect, regardless of their background.

words.definition: to mention a topic in conversation
words.example: She brought up an interesting idea during the meeting.

words.definition: to form an opinion based on the information available
words.example: After reading the article, draw a conclusion about the author's message.

words.definition: to have an effect on something; to be involved
words.example: Parents play a role in their children's education.

words.definition: to cause something to happen
words.example: The new law brought about important changes in education.

words.definition: to decide that something is not possible
words.example: The detective ruled out two suspects based on the evidence.

words.definition: to cause something to happen
words.example: Eating too much sugar can lead to health problems.

words.definition: using something as the reason or foundation
words.example: The winner was chosen on the basis of creativity and effort.

words.definition: before a particular time or event
words.example: Finish your homework prior to watching television.

words.definition: with regard to a particular aspect
words.example: In terms of speed, the cheetah is the fastest land animal.

words.definition: to end up being; to have a particular result
words.example: The science project turned out better than expected.

words.definition: to say that something is caused by a particular thing
words.example: Scientists attribute the warming temperatures to greenhouse gases.
